titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Janet Malcolm Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday October 24, 2019 at 03:11

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of research materials, writings, correspondence, vertical files, printed material, audiovisual materials, and electronic media documenting the work and writings of Janet Malcolm. Research materials and writings consist of correspondence, legal documents, transcripts, notes, manuscripts, drafts, proofs, printed material, and other papers created or collected by Janet Malcolm in the course of researching or writing various projects including Psychoanalysis: The Impossible Profession (Alfred A. Knopf, 1981), In the Freud Archives (Alfred A. Knopf, 1984), The Journalist and the Murderer (Alfred A. Knopf, 1990), The Silent Woman: Sylvia Plath and Ted Hughes (Alfred A. Knopf, 1994), The Crime of Sheila McGough (Alfred A. Knopf, 1999), and others. Research materials also include files of correspondence and related papers from J. D. Salinger and Phillip Roth. Vertical files consist of additional writings, correspondence, notebooks, and research materials. Audiovisual materials consist of audiocassettes containing interviews and other recordings generated by Malcolm while conducting research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 55-58 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 59 (digital media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Research materials and writings, 1950-2010. II. Vertical files, 1940-2009. III. Printed materials, 1964-2007. IV. Audiovisual materials, circa 1970s-2005. V. Electronic media, circa 1990s.

bioghist.head: Janet Malcolm
bioghist.p: Janet Malcolm (born 1934) is an American writer and journalist. A longtime staff writer for The New Yorker and author of numerous books, Malcolm has written on a range of subjects including photography, psychoanalysis, Sylvia Plath and Ted Hughes, and Gertrude Stein and Alice B. Toklas.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Janet Malcolm on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2013.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Janet Malcom Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Janet Malcolm Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Research materials and writings are arranged roughly by project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Research materials and writings consist correspondence, legal documents, transcripts, notes, manuscripts, drafts, proofs, printed material, and other papers created or collected by Janet Malcolm in the course of researching or writing various projects. They document the following works: Psychoanalysis: The Impossible Profession (Alfred A. Knopf, 1981), In the Freud Archives (Alfred A. Knopf, 1984), The Journalist and the Murderer (Alfred A. Knopf, 1990), The Silent Woman: Sylvia Plath and Ted Hughes (Alfred A. Knopf, 1994), The Crime of Sheila McGough (Alfred A. Knopf, 1999), Reading Chekhov: A Critical Journey (Random House, 2001), Two Lives: Gertrude and Alice (Yale University Press, 2007), and Iphigenia in Forest Hills: Anatomy of a Murder Trial (Yale University Press, 2011). Also includes research files on the Supreme Court, Ingri Sischy, and other topics, as well as files of correspondence and related papers from J. D. Salinger and Phillip Roth.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: For related materials, see also Series II. Vertical files.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, writings by others including correspondence with J.D. Salinger material and Philip Roth material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, research material, photographs, writings, correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, notes and printed material that includes Gargoyle and The New Republic.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Janet Malcolm's vertical files were kept in two filing units with three and four drawers, respectively. The arrangement of the files maintains their order by file and drawer as organized by Malcolm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Vertical files consists of incoming and outgoing correspondence, writings, writings by others, notebooks, and other papers relating to Janet Malcolm's research and writings.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: For related materials, see also Series I. Research materials and writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence by fax and letter includes incoming and outgoing and additional materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings, drafts, interviews, notebooks and correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed materials consist of copies of the New York Review of Books and newspaper and magazine clippings saved by Janet Malcolm.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issues with contributions by Janet Malcom.

arrangement.p: Arranged roughly in the order in which they were received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Audiovisual materials consist of audiocassettes containing interviews and other recordings generated by Janet Malcolm while conducting research.

did.materialspec: The program begins and ends abruptly. The program is distorted at times. The audio, at times, may sound hollow or unclear. The first 47 minutes was recorded at a lower level.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.p: The program begins abruptly. High background noise can be heard.

did.materialspec: The program begins and ends abruptly. High background noise can be heard. Audio, at times, can sound hollow or unclear.

accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Sheila & Irene E[?] End; Sheila Sunday End
did.materialspec: High background noise can be heard on the tape. The program is distorted at times. Audio, at times, may sound hollow or unclear. The right channel was recorded at a lower level.

did.materialspec: High background noise can be heard. There is a gap in the program from 47:40-48:04. The program ends abruptly.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: The program was recorded at a very low level. There is a buzz on the tape. Static can be heard. High background noise can be heard. The program is distorted at times. The right channel of audio ws recorded at a lower level. Audio, at times, sounds hollow or unclear. The program ends abruptly.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: The right channel's audio was recorded at a lower level. High background noise can be heard. The program is distorted at times. Static can be heard.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: Static can be heard. High background noise can be heard. The program is distorted at times. The right channel was recorded at a lower level for much of the tape.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: The program begins abruptly. The program is distorted at times. The right channel was recorded at a lower level. High background noise can be heard. Heavy distortion and interference can be heard beginning at 13:05.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: The program is distorted at times. High background noise can be heard. There are occasional dropouts in the audio throughout the tape. The right channel was recorded at a lower level. The program ends abruptly.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: High background noise can be heard. The right channel was recorded at a lower level. The right channel level decreases gradually for the first 48 minutes.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: A high amount of buzz can be heard. Static is on the tape. The program was distorted at times. There are low levels in the audio from the beginning of the tape to about 25 minutes in. Low-level cross talk can be heard at times.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: The program is distorted at times. Static and a high amount of buzz can be heard. Low-level cross talk can be heard at times.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: A high amount of buzz can be heard on the tape. Static is present in the program. Backwards content can be heard in the middle of the program. Backwards sections on the tape were reversed in post-production from 22:02-49:05 and 52:45-01:23:29. The program ends abruptly.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: The right channel was recorded at a lower level. The program is distorted at times. High background noise can be heard. There is a gap in the program from 54:37-55:02. The program ends abruptly. Static can be heard.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: The program begins and ends abruptly. High background noise can be heard. Static is also present.
